ab: ta uzusa melalazan uzusa betin yau uzusa tasaw, pasaya ti sihu tu huygi simsanu qanengi ma tu sudadan na yencumincu semudad tu nangan ta mibunsiwan na yencumin.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: pasanu iku tu malemes ay semuzuq temunuR tu sihu qanengi “wan ma semudad tu nangan na yencumin” ta mibunsiwan, sining na damu ti angaw ateng.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年angaw ateng。
en: I interviewed angaw ateng, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: semiqet iku timaizipana ta hibangan na lipay zau qatiw sa leppaw na laut pasanu timaizipana.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: ta pasanuan zau nani, paqesuppaR aizipna timaiku manna sinangan aizipna tu nangan zau, pasimqananam timaiku tu pinileppaw na seRay damu na.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: mipil iku timaizipana, “manna ngid isu uman tu nangan, masang ay waway temuki tu nangan, sukaw ni?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: semanu aizipna timaiku, anu izan na sudadan na hulam semudad tu nangan na, maspalaki ti, yauzuma yau qaya ’daq qumRas timaizipana tu “阿先生”, paqetawan.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「阿先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him “Aa Xian Sheng,” which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: semanu ti angaw ateng, “izan ta zata ay sudadan qawka maken ay nangan ta. nangan ku nani, yau waway na lazatan niaq, leppaw niaq, tungus niaq tu lazatan, ngid qazemun pa Raqit ta patadikud tu nangan na.”
ch: angaw ateng說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: angaw ateng said, “The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.”
